title: Baccharis braunii
intro: Baccharis braunii, also known as Palo Amarillo or Yellow Sage, is a shrub native to the Andean region, belonging to the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of fever, colds, and respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Baccharis braunii include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, Baccharis braunii is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as being incorporated into salves and creams for topical application.
taxonomy: Baccharis braunii is classified as a species within the genus Baccharis, which is a group of flowering plants in the family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family, comprising over 23,000 species. The Baccharis genus is native to the Americas, with many species found in South America. Baccharis braunii is specifically native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It belongs to the order Asterales, a group of flowering plants with a characteristic inflorescence structure.
uses: Baccharis braunii is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various cultures for its medicinal properties. Its leaves and stems have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's leaves are also rich in antioxidants and have been reported to have anti-inflammatory properties. Baccharis braunii has been used in traditional medicine to treat wounds and skin conditions due to its antimicrobial and antiseptic properties. Further research is needed to fully understand the plant's potential medicinal uses and benefits.

preparations: Baccharis braunii, also known as the silver broom baccharis, has been used in traditional herbal medicine for various purposes.
